# Aletheia

> Self-hosted multi-agent AI runtime with persistent memory, tools, HTTP/SSE, Signal, TUI, and desktop operator surfaces.

Aletheia runs named nous agents with durable memory, domain packs, tool execution, and local-first knowledge. The runtime is a Rust workspace with one main binary and embedded storage; configured LLM providers are the only required external dependency.

## Binary

`aletheia` - the main CLI and service binary. It starts the HTTP/SSE gateway by default and includes commands for init, health, status, backup, config, memory, eval, sessions, imports, exports, and TUI launch.

## Architecture

Structured LLM corpus:
- [_llm/architecture.toml](_llm/architecture.toml): workspace layers, crate roles, and interfaces
- [_llm/current_state.toml](_llm/current_state.toml): phase, recent work, and active threads
- [_llm/decisions.toml](_llm/decisions.toml): accepted runtime and architecture decisions
- [_llm/glossary.toml](_llm/glossary.toml): canonical aletheia vocabulary
- [_llm/apis.toml](_llm/apis.toml): CLI, MCP, and HTTP surfaces

## Agent Entry Points

- [README.md](README.md): product overview and install path
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): repo conventions and working instructions
- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): dependency graph and trait boundaries
- [docs/TECHNOLOGY.md](docs/TECHNOLOGY.md): technology decision rationale
- [instance.example/nous/_template/](instance.example/nous/_template/): template agent workspace
